problem: (configureProcessGroup) Cancel returns non-ESRCH kill errors directly to exec.CommandContext, which ignores cancel errors and proceeds to Wait; the kill error is discarded and never reaches the caller, so a failed group kill (e.g. EPERM from a privilege-dropped child) is silent and indistinguishable from a clean kill
fix: Return nil on non-ESRCH errors so the cancel is acknowledged but don't surface the kill failure; surface the failure separately via a result field (e.g. ReapError) or log it at minimum
evidence: Cancel returns err directly; exec.CommandContext.cancel does not propagate cancel errors
justification: HIGH|internal/verify/localvalidate_pgroup_unix.go:26|Cancel returns non-ESRCH kill errors directly to exec.CommandContext, which ignores cancel errors and proceeds to Wait; the kill error is discarded and never reaches the caller, so a failed group kill (e.g. EPERM from a privilege-dropped child) is silent and indistinguishable from a clean kill|Return nil on non-ESRCH errors so the cancel is acknowledged but don't surface the kill failure; surface the failure separately via a result field (e.g. ReapError) or log it at minimum|error-handling|20|Cancel returns err directly; exec.CommandContext.cancel does not propagate cancel errors

problem: (configureProcessGroup) The group SIGKILL fires only when runCtx.Done() triggers Cancel; if the command exits 0 or non-zero before the timeout, Cancel never fires and any orphaned grandchildren survive indefinitely
fix: Acceptable within the timeout-only epic scope but leaves a leak path if a validation command backgrounds children and exits; document the limitation explicitly
evidence: No mechanism for the clean-exit case
justification: HIGH|internal/verify/localvalidate_pgroup_unix.go:26|Cancel returns non-ESRCH kill errors directly to exec.CommandContext, which ignores cancel errors and proceeds to Wait; the kill error is discarded and never reaches the caller, so a failed group kill (e.g. EPERM from a privilege-dropped child) is silent and indistinguishable from a clean kill|Return nil on non-ESRCH errors so the cancel is acknowledged but don't surface the kill failure; surface the failure separately via a result field (e.g. ReapError) or log it at minimum|error-handling|20|Cancel returns err directly; exec.CommandContext.cancel does not propagate cancel errors

problem: (TestRunConfiguredValidation_TimeoutReapsGrandchild) processAlive uses bare PID without any uniqueness guarantee across the 5s Eventually window — OS PID recycling can flip the assertion to a false-negative failure or, if a new process happens to appear at that PID, a false-positive pass
fix: Use kill(-pid, 0) to probe the whole group rather than a single recycled PID, or capture the child start-time via os.FindProcess and compare to confirm it's the original process
evidence: [dax] return syscall.Kill(pid, syscall.Signal(0)) != syscall.ESRCH / [mira] processAlive probes a single PID that may be recycled
justification:
LOW|internal/verify/localvalidate_pgroup_unix.go:29|Non-ESRCH errors from syscall.Kill (e.g., EPERM if a group member drops privileges) propagate up through cmd.Run() and bypass the TimedOut branch in RunConfiguredValidation, masking a failed reap as a clean timeout|Log or record the error when it is neither nil nor ESRCH so RunConfiguredValidation can surface reap failures instead of masking them|OBSERVABILITY|15|err := syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL) ... if err == syscall.ESRCH { return os.ErrProcessDone } return err
LOW|internal/verify/localvalidate_pgroup_unix_test.go:47|processAlive relies on the reaped grandchild PID not being recycled by the OS within the 5s Eventually window; PID reuse flips the assertion to a false-negative failure|Accept the low probability as documented, or additionally probe the whole group (kill(-pid, 0)) or capture the child start time to detect reuse|EDGE_CASES|15|return syscall.Kill(pid, syscall.Signal(0)) != syscall.ESRCH
MEDIUM|interna…

problem: (configureProcessGroup) errors.Is not used — bare `err == syscall.ESRCH` works today but breaks if Kill wraps the error in a wrapped sentinel (e.g. os.(*PathError) wrapping syscall.Errno)
fix: Use errors.Is(err, syscall.ESRCH) and add a comment that this must track any future wrapping
evidence: [mira] bare == comparison on line 33 / [otto] if err == syscall.ESRCH
